sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: 39g of fat. saturated fat is a major pressure point. for meats, fat quality matters a lot once the protein is already there.

sections.title: Proteins
sections.narration: 12.5g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. strong protein is expected here, so the rest of the profile decides how high it climbs.

sections.narration: pros first: highly palatable and appealing because a real-world reason people love it. works as a strong flavour booster because useful in meals even in smaller amounts. has strong comfort-food appeal because popular and instantly legible for content packaging.

closing.summary: Bacon does have a few real-world upsides. but the nutrition case is still weak and the downsides drag it down hard. best only if convenience, budget, or taste matters more than getting the cleanest meat option.
